# UC-17.1.79 · ISE Authorization-Policy Funnel — From Match to Final Authorization Result

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Operations, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When users connect, ISE checks a long list of rules to decide what they're allowed to do. We map out which rules are used and which aren't — so the team can clean up unused rules and tighten access.*

---

## Description

Tracks how authentications flow through the policy stack. Identifies (a) policy rules that never match (dead rules), (b) catch-all rules that match too often (suggesting more specific rules are missed), (c) authorization profiles that are configured but never assigned.

## Value

Authorization policies grow over time and accumulate dead rules and catch-all over-grants. Funnel visualization gives policy-engineering teams data-driven input to prune rules and tighten access.

## Implementation

Schedule the search daily over the last 7 days; review the funnel-drop chart in the ISE Authorization Funnel dashboard.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable a policy rule (or set conditions impossible). Funnel shows zero match volume after change.

### Negative scenario

Active policies. Funnel shows expected distribution.

## Detailed Implementation

Standard pattern.

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (5200, 5236)
| stats count, dc(user) as users by MatchedPolicyName, MatchedRuleName, AuthorizationProfile
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Sankey diagram: rules → profiles → SGTs/dACLs/VLANs. Dead-rule list. Catch-all rule top-N.

## Known False Positives

- Newly added rules — match volume builds up over weeks.
- Disaster-recovery catch-all rules legitimately match in failure modes.

## References

- [Cisco ISE 3.4 — Authorization Policies](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/)
